title: Polystichum nymphale
intro: Polystichum nymphale, also known as Nephrolepis, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Polystichum nymphale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the form of tincture or dried extract in herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Polystichum nymphale is classified as a species within the genus Polystichum and the family Dryopteridaceae. This classification places it among the true ferns. Polystichum nymphale is further categorized as a species of the subfamily Dryopteridoideae. As a member of the Polystichum genus, it is also classified as a species of the order Polypodiales. The specific classification of Polystichum nymphale is subject to ongoing research and potential updates in the field of botany.
